macro_summary: On Tuesday, stocks were mixed as a Trump-brokered deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz sent oil plunging 6.6% to $75.37, but the Nasdaq fell 1.75% as AI-linked chips sold off ahead of Wednesday’s FOMC meeting. WTI at a three‑month low eased energy‑driven inflation fears, yet the ECB and Fed warned that damaged supply infrastructure could keep price pressures elevated for months. The S&P 500 slipped 0.48% while SOXX tumbled 5.64%, dragging semis broadly lower.
commodities_note: WTI cratered 6.6% to $75.37 – its lowest since March – after Trump confirmed an agreement with Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Gold was flat at $4,351.4, DXY dipped 0.2%. The US 10‑year yield fell to 4.43%, reflecting reduced rate‑hike bets. The first Fed meeting chaired by Kevin Warsh on Wednesday will test whether the committee pencils in any hikes.

INTC at 81.6x forward PE prices in a foundry revenue ramp that hasn't started; FCF yield is negative.

rcp_state.terms_summary: PRESS-REPORTED (not yet Intel-IR-confirmed): Alphabet to make ~3M in-house chips via Intel foundry (The Information / Reuters, 2026-06-09), + Nvidia foundry buzz + Hitachi pact. Drove the +11.2% gap; price corroborated ($110 -> ~$124 by 06-13). Append to rcp_invalidation_events if the deal is denied.

writeup.headline: Gap‑fill failure kills the Google foundry re‑rate; back to CAUTION.
writeup.what_to_do: Intel’s 18A‑P entered risk production (per Reuters, Jun 16), but the alleged Google foundry deal that gapped the stock on June 9 has already been completely retraced. The RCP state machine officially failed: the gap filled in just two sessions. Without confirmed external customers, the foundry thesis is unproven. 18A‑P’s incremental performance gains are real, but the 19% extension above the 50‑day and a negative FCF yield make this a no‑entry zone. A full reset below $100 would put the P/B floor in play, but not before.

writeup.headline: Upgraded to ACCUMULATE — R:R 4.2:1, MEMS timing thesis primes at the 50‑day.
writeup.what_to_do: SiTime corrected 7.8% on Tuesday, pulling back from extreme extension to 8.1% above the 50‑day at 633.32. The MEMS‑timing displacement thesis is intact and the wide‑stop R:R to 609.15 is 2.86:1, clearing our gate. Entry zone 655‑685, invalidation at 633.32 (50‑day reclaim failure). Upside to 901.81 represents the first measured swing. Half position — the thesis rests on a single NVDA‑dependent design‑win leg. Starter sizing appropriate until a second independent programme is disclosed. [paper]
writeup.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 64x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.
writeup.thesis_break_condition: Thesis breaks if SITM reverts to CAUTION on the next session — the signal upgrade requires confirmation.

support_legs: NVDA GPU cluster synchronization drives MEMS timing adoption at scale
Legacy quartz oscillator displacement in 400G/800G datacenter networking is a multi‑year secular shift

singapore.summary: All three banks are now hard‑blocked on extension and overbought, as US10Y at 4.43% remains far above the 4.20% NIM‑relief threshold. No price action in the cluster offers an entry — the wait for a pullback to 50‑day lines is measured in weeks. OCBC’s relative profit growth is the differentiator, but the setup doesn’t favor any one name.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically blocked — NIM compression must ease before banks become actionable.
singapore.key_development: None today; the cluster is in a holding pattern as the Hormuz de‑escalation oil drop marginally lowers global rate pressure but MAS’s S$NEER stance holds.

semiconductors.summary: The semis cluster took a sharp -5.6% hit as the Iran peace deal’s initial pop faded and rate anxiety ahead of the Fed meeting weighed. AMD, INTC, ASML, MU, and 000660.KS all remain deep in extension territory; NVDA slipped back to HOLD, and only SITM graduates to ACCUMULATE on a pullback to its 50‑day. The cluster’s median is 30‑40% extended, making stock‑picking treacherous — until a broad reset drops several names into high‑quality entry zones, the lever is sector beta, not individual selection.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish but tactically extended — the AI buildout cycle is real, but price has outrun fundamentals in most names.
semiconductors.key_development: Intel’s 18A‑P progression is positive, but the Google foundry re‑rate completely failed; that failure reinforces that the cluster is pricing in deals before they are confirmed.

big_tech.summary: BigTech is increasingly bifurcated: GOOG is the only name in a near‑actionable setup, while AMZN and MSFT are stuck below their 50‑day lines with capex/credit‑circularity overhangs. PLTR’s European contract losses are a fresh thesis impairment. With the Fed decision tomorrow, the entire cluster could move on Warsh’s dot‑plot; no entries ahead of that binary.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — GOOG’s TPU strength resists the broader AI caution, but AMZN/MSFT remain structurally impaired until capex ROI or Azure ex‑OpenAI metrics appear.
big_tech.key_development: Microsoft’s resort to competing clouds for AI capacity is a rare admission of supply constraints that could dent AI narrative credibility.

interconnected.name: Coherent Corp.
interconnected.reason: Received NVIDIA’s matching $2B photonics investment alongside LITE — direct read‑through to AI datacenter optical interconnect demand.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if Coherent pulls back 15‑20% from the recent extension, or if LITE posts a positive earnings catalyst that lifts the photonics sector.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC and datacenter networking chip designer; competes with AVGO in AI custom silicon, directly benefits from hyperscaler capex acceleration.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after MU/AMD earnings in late June for memory and GPU demand signals; a pullback to the 200‑day would make it attractive.

interconnected.name: Cadence Design Systems
interconnected.reason: EDA software leader whose export restrictions to China mirror ASML’s risk — a pure‑play on the US‑China tech supply chain decoupling.
interconnected.entry_note: Becomes interesting if new semiconductor equipment export bans are proposed; watch for any congressional hearing announcements.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: Leading datacenter switch vendor; directly tied to AI GPU cluster networking and NVDA Spectrum‑X ecosystem — a beneficiary of every GPU deployment.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if NVIDIA delivers a strong Blackwell Ultra volume guide that implies a switch‑infrastructure upgrade cycle.

interconnected.name: Snowflake
interconnected.reason: AI data cloud platform whose consumption model correlates with hyperscaler AI workload growth — a downstream software play on AI capex.
interconnected.entry_note: Watch after GOOG/MSFT Q2 prints for cloud revenue acceleration; a 20% correction from current levels would align with past buying opportunities.

active_risks: US‑Iran Hormuz deal signed; oil prices collapse but ECB/Fed warn recovery of supply infrastructure will take months, keeping inflation sticky.
FOMC meeting tomorrow with new Chair Kevin Warsh — analysts expect a hold, but a minority may pencil in a hike, adding hawkish tail risk.
China export surge and Trump’s tariff escalation broaden US‑China tech conflict beyond semiconductors; Ford and others seek licenses for connected‑car software.
geopolitical.market_impact: The oil crash temporarily depresses energy‑linked inflation but the Fed is unlikely to pivot before Wednesday’s meeting. Tech stocks are under pressure from both rate anxiety and the MSFT cloud capacity headline, dragging NVDA, AMD, and AVGO lower. Semis cluster is broadly extended; Singapore banks hold on NIM relief hopes but remain blocked.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: President Trump announced a completed US‑Iran agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz; WTI fell below $80 for the first time since March. FOMC preview hints at a hawkish hold, with a few dots potentially signaling hikes. ECB’s Nagel and Lane warned energy‑driven inflation will persist despite the peace deal.

base.description: FOMC holds rates, Warsh signals a prolonged pause; WTI stabilizes around $75‑85 as supply recovers gradually. Semis cluster remains extended but avoids a crash; watches for earnings catalysts. NVDA recovers above its 50‑day; selective WATCH signals become actionable after the Fed.

optimistic.description: Hormuz reopening accelerates and energy infrastructure damage is less than feared; inflation eases sharply, and the Fed hints at a possible cut later in 2026. Oil falls to $65‑70; tech stocks rally broadly, and the Singapore cluster sees NIM relief as US10Y drops sustainably below 4.20%. ACCUMULATE candidates multiply.

pessimistic.description: FOMC dot plot includes multiple hikes; US10Y spikes above 4.60% and oil supply recovery stalls due to damaged assets. Tech and semis sell off anew; Singapore NIM pressure deepens. CAUTION signals hold, and several names (MSFT, PLTR) tip into AVOID on fresh impairments.

wildcard.description: OpenAI’s leaked financials ($38.5B loss) trigger a credit event or antitrust action that hits MSFT, dragging cloud/AI names. Combined with a hawkish Fed surprise, the Nasdaq falls 10‑15% in a week; the entire watchlist is underwater, and no new positions are possible.

geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold current positions; no new entries ahead of Wednesday’s FOMC. The peace rally is not yet confirmed by inflation data or central bank signals.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Cautiously bullish — macro risks are easing but technical extension blocks 14 of 29 names.
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 14 of 29 names are hard‑blocked on technical extension, with semis and Singapore banks leading the congestion. The FOMC decision tomorrow is the binary that could reset entry zones if hawkish, or keep us waiting if dovish.

scheduled_tech_events.notes: Vera Rubin samples confirmed shipping; optics integration revealed in Rubin rack design. Blackwell Ultra → Rubin transition timeline given.
Taiwan AI infrastructure partnerships; CoWoS capacity commitments from TSMC; HBM4 roadmap. Direct thesis-confirming event for AI Power Infrastructure cluster.
AI kit, on-device model integration, Vision Pro 2 rumours. Competitive signal for MSFT Copilot and GOOG Gemini on-device.

accumulate.note: Enter 655‑685, invalidation at 633.32 (50‑day). R:R 4.2:1 to 901.81. Half position — single MEMS‑timing leg; starter sizing. ACCUMULATE -- starter position.

watch_for_entry.note: R:R 3.7:1 but wide‑stop math fails. Upgrades to WATCH above 404.23 on volume. Stop below 362.22.
Upgraded from CAUTION; pullback to 50‑day at 12.99 is the entry zone.
Entry zone 2,112‑2,160 TWD; bounce from 50‑day on volume triggers. Wide‑stop math still low.

regime_change_pending.note: Oracle Jupiter gap consolidation session 9/25. Path A retest of new SMA50 (234.5) or Path B breakout above ~293 are the graduation triggers. No entry until graduation.

Azure capacity crunch is a near‑term headwind, but non‑OpenAI RPO +28% is the cleaner signal. Reassess after the FOMC or a reclaim of 412.

GaN promise is real but the stock needs a pullback to the 50‑day at 19.53 to become tradable.

macro_trigger_map.event: FOMC Rate Decision (Warsh’s first press conference)
macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: Hold rates, Warsh signals prolonged pause and dot plot shows no hikes; US10Y falls below 4.3%, tech rallies, VIX subsides.
bullish_upgrades: GOOG → ACCUMULATE candidate (if volume confirms breakout)
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: Dot plot includes one or two hikes; US10Y spikes above 4.6%, semis sell off, extension blocks deepen.
bearish_impact: Semis cluster → multiple names flip to AVOID on thesis impairment

taxonomy_discrimination.note: Output-only meta-analysis of signal-class discrimination. NOT injected into the analysis prompt and NOT persisted into memory.lessons_learned. full_corpus pools pre-cutover (different-instrument) rows — informational only.

calibration_insights.confidence_banner: Actionable buckets BUY (n=2) / ACCUMULATE (n=7) spanning trend_up. NOT yet decision-grade — alpha figures are benchmark-relative; single-regime samples carry no out-of-regime evidence.
calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop. Treat as a reader-facing self-knowledge summary.
